text.title: Blameless Retro Template (Human Facilitation) Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Retro ends with one-line 'root cause: X' + one action item. Two months later same incident class recurs.
antipattern.root-cause: Team latches onto a single explanation and stops looking; meeting time underbudgeted for contributing-factors block.
antipattern.fix: Facilitator explicitly asks 'what made this go undetected?', 'what made impact bigger?', 'what made recovery slower?' — each opens a new factor.

antipattern.symptom: Factors + action items still name specific people ('Bob's deploy', 'Alex's mistake'); blame framing not enforced in-meeting.
antipattern.root-cause: Language was not interrupted in real-time; post-meeting cleanup attempted but doesn't change behaviour.
antipattern.fix: Facilitator coach session — practice reframes. For current retro, rewrite the named entries as system-as-subject before publishing.

antipattern.symptom: Retro generates 15+ action items, no priority, none complete.
antipattern.root-cause: No top-3 selection at end; backlog created equal to action-items list.
antipattern.fix: Force 'top 3 by impact' selection. Others move to backlog WITHOUT owners (visible, uncommitted).

antipattern.symptom: Time pressure → facilitator skips meta round; highest-leverage insights die in the room.
antipattern.root-cause: Contributing-factors block ran over; meta perceived as 'optional'.
antipattern.fix: Time-box contributing-factors aggressively. Meta round 10 min, non-skippable. If pressed, end action-items block early instead.
